question: Who issues Ordinances?
explanation: According to Article 123 of Constitution of India, President have power to promulgate ordinances during recess of parliament that means when both the houses or either of the house in not in session ordinance will be passed with the recommendation of the Union Cabinet. Its duration is up to 6 weeks after the passing of the ordinance. It is mandatory to appear in the parliament within 6 months, if this is not done, then it is canceled its entire effect ends. Note : Article 213 empowers the Governor to pass ordinances.

question: Who presides over the Joint Sittings of the Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha?

options.a: The Speaker of the Lok Sabha
options.b: The Vice President as Chairman of the Rajya Sabha
options.d: A senior MP nominated by the President

explanation: The joint sitting of the Parliament is called by the President of India (Aticle 108) and is presided over by the Speaker of Lok Sabha or, in their absence, by the Deputy Speaker of the Lok Sabha, or in their absence, by the Chairman, or by the absence the Deputy Chairman of the Rajya Sabha. Any of the above officers are not present, then any other member of the Parliament can preside by consensus of both the House.

question: As per the Constitution of India, the term of the members of ‘Council of States’ is for :
explanation: The ‘Council of States’ which is also known as Rajya Sabha, a nomenclature that was announced by the chair in the House on the 23rd August, 1954. The origin of the second Chamber can be traced to the Montague-Chelmsford Report of 1918. Article 80 of the Constitution lays down the maximum strength of Rajya Sabha as 250, out of which 12 members are nominated by the President and 238 are representatives of the States and of the two Union Territories. The Fourth Schedule to the Constitution provides for allocation of seats to the States and Union Territories in Rajya Sabha. The allocation of seats is made on the basis of the population of each State. Rajya Sabha is a permanent House and is not subject to dissolution. However, One-Third Members of Rajya Sabha retire after every second year. A member who is elected for a full term serves for a period of six years. Polity

question: Who decides whether a bill is a Money Bill or not?
explanation: Speaker of the Lok Sabha decides whether a Bill is a Money Bill or not. Article 110 of the Constitution deals with the money bills. A money bill can be introduced only in Lok Sabha. President’s recommendation is required to introduce a Money Bill.

question: The maximum strength of members of the Lok Sabha is
explanation: The maximum strength of the House envisaged by the Constitution is 552, which is made up by election of up to 530 members to represent the States, up to 20 members to represent the Union Territories and not more than 2 members of the Anglo-Indian Community to be nominated by Honorable President of India. Article 81–Talks about House of the People or Lok Sabha. Note:- By the 104th Amendment Act 2020, The reserved seat for the Anglo Indian community is removed in the Lok Sabha and State Assembly

question: Among the total members of Rajya Sabha, how many of them are nominated members?
explanation: Under Article 80 of the Constitution, the council of states (Rajay Sabha) is composed of not more than 250 members, of whom 12 are nominated by the President of India from amongst persons who have special knowledge or practical experience in matters such as Literature, Science, Art and Social Service. • The present strength of Rajya Sabha, however is 245, out of which 233 are representatives of the states and Union Territories and 12 are nominated by the President.

question: After how many year, do one-third of the members of the Rajya Sabha retire?
explanation: Rajya Sabha is a permanent body and is not subject to dissolution. However, one third of the members retire every second year, and are replaced by newly elected members. Each member is elected for a term of six years. The Vice President of India is the ex– officio Chairman of Rajya Sabha.

question: The Indian Constitution provides that the Rajya Sabha shall consist of not more than ................. members.
explanation: Rajya Sabha is also known as the upper house of the Parliament Article 80 lays down the maximum strength of Rajya Sabha is 250 in which 238 members represent states and Union Territories and 12 member nominated by the President from Art, Literature, Science and Social Service Field.

question: A candidate for the office of the President of India should not be less than
explanation: • Minimum age for becoming President and Governor – 35 years • Minimum age for becoming Chief Minister and Prime Minister – 25 years • Minimum age for becoming member of Rajya Sabha – 30 years

question: Majority of the Members of the Rajya Sabha are

explanation: The money bill (Article 110), is concerned with financial matters like-Taxation, Public Expenditure etc. (cid:1) A money bill can be introduced only in Lok Sabha and can be introduced only on the recommendation of the President. explanation: There are 250 representative in the Council of States in which 12 are nominated by the President from various fields. So, there are 238 elected representatives in the upper house. Rajya Sabha is the upper house of the Parliament and its tenure is 6 years.

question: Who among the following got the honour of becoming the first speaker of the Lok Sabha?

question: Article 80 of the Constitution of India lays down the maximum strength of Rajya Sabha as:
explanation: Article 80 of the Constitution lays down the maximum strength of Rajya Sabha as 250. Out of 250 seats of Rajya Sabha, 12 members are nominated by the President and 238 are representatives of the States and of the two Union Territories. The present strength of Rajya Sabha is 245, out of which 233 are representatives of the States and Union territories of Delhi and Puducherry and 12 are nominated by the President. The members nominated by the President are persons having special knowledge or practical experience in respect of such matters as literature, science, art and social service.

question: The period between the prorogation of a House and its reassembly in a new session is called _____.
explanation: The period between the prorogation of a house and its reassembly in a new session is called Recess.

question: Which of the following Articles of the Constitution of India makes provisions for a joint sitting of both Houses of Parliament?
explanation: Article 108 of the India Constitution provides for a joint sitting of both Houses of Parliament. The joint session is summoned by the President. It is presided over by the speaker of the Lok Sabha. The joint sitting is governed by the rules of procedure of Lok Sabha and not of Rajya Sabha.

question: Who is the Ex-Officio chairman of the Rajya Sabha?

question: Vote of no-confidence motion is brought in which house?
explanation: In India a motion of no-confidence can be introduced only in the Lok Sabha (the lower house of the Parliament of India). The motion is admitted for discussion when a minimum of 50 members of the House support the motion. If the motion carries, the House debates and votes on the motion. If a majority of the motion is passed and the government is bound to vacate the office. • The no-confidence motion is not mentioned in the Indian Constitution.

question: Which of the following statements is not correct?

question: Who is current speaker of Rajya Sabha?
explanation: Currently Rajya Sabha Speaker is M. Venkaiah Naidu since 11 August 2017. Deputy Chairman – Harivansh Narayan Singh since 14 September 2020.

question: The Council of States (Rajya Sabha) was first constituted in the year :
explanation: The Council of States also known as Rajya Sabha was first constituted on 03 April 1952. • Dr. S. Radhakrishnan was the first chairman of the Rajya Sabha. • Shri S.V. Krishnamoorthy Rao was the first deputy chairman of Rajya Sabha.

question: Which of the following statements is/are correct about Zero Hour?

options.a: Zero Hour has special provision mentioned in the rule of procedure
options.b: India has adopted Zero Hour from UK
options.c: Zero Hour starts immediately after the Question Hour
options.d: Before raising question in Zero Hour the members of parliament required to give prior notice

explanation: Zero Hour starts immediately after the Question Hour. The time immediately following the question hour has come to be known as "Zero Hour". It starts at around 12 Noon and members can, raise matter without prior notice to the Speaker, raise issues of importance during this time. It is neither mentioned in the Constitution nor rules of procedure and contract of business in Lok Sabha. It enables the MPs to address important issue of public importance.

question: Assertion (A): The device of Adjournment motion is not used by the Rajya Sabha Reason (R) : An Adjournment motion does not result in removing the Government from office

options.a: Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A)
options.b: Both (A) and (R) are true but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A)

explanation: Adjournment motion is introduced only in the Lok Sabha to draw the attention of the House to a definite matter of urgent public importance. • An adjournment motion does not result in removing the Government from office.

question: Which of the following States sends the maximum number of members to the Rajya Sabha?
explanation: Uttar Pradesh sends maximum 31 number of member to the Rajya Sabha or Council of States. Membership of Rajya Sabha is limited by the Constitution to a maximum of 250 members and current laws have provision for 245 members. It is upper House of the Parliament of India. President of India nominates 12 members to Rajya Sabha from various fields like Art, Literature etc.

question: Who among the following was never the Lok Sabha Speaker?

options.b: People of Indian as a whole

explanation: Lok Sabha represents the people of India where as Rajya Sabha represents the state of Indian Federation. The Lok Sabha, is the lower house of the Parliament of India. As of 2019, there have been seventeenth Lok Sabha's, elected by the people of India. The Maximum membership of the House allotted by the Constitution of India is 550 (Initially, in 1950, it was 500). Currently, the house has 543 members.

question: Who was the first woman to be nominated as the member of Rajya Sabha?
explanation: Rukmini Devi Arundale was an Indian theosophist, dancer and choreographer of the Indian classical dance form of Bharatnatyam, and an activist for animal rights & welfare. She was also the first ever woman in Indian history to be nominated as a member to the Rajya Sabha. Sucheta Kripalani was an Indian freedom fighter and India's first woman Chief Minister serving as the head of the Uttar Pradesh Government. Rajkumari Bibiji Amrit Kaur was appointed the first Health Minister of India in 1947 and remained in office until 1957. Kamaladevi Chattopadhyay was the first lady in India to stand in elections from Madras constituency. Polity

question: Which is the only Union Territory from which a member is elected to the Rajya Sabha?
explanation: One seat of Rajya Sabha has been allotted for Puducherry. Uttar Pradesh has the highest number of Rajya Sabha seats. 3 seats have been allocated for the National Capital Delhi.

question: The quorum of Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha is
explanation: For quorum, the tenth part (1/10) of the total members of the house is required to be present in the house. Thus quorum in the Rajya Sabha and Lok Sabha is from 25 and 55 members respectively. Note : Quorum is necessary to initiate the proceedings of the Houses of Parliament. If at any time there is no quorum of a House of Parliament, the Speaker or the Deputy Speaker may adjourn the proceedings of the House until the quorum of the House is done.

question: Consider following statements about 'Money Bills': 1. Both the Houses have equal rights on them. 2. Rajya Sabha can recommend Amendment in it. 3. It originates from Rajya Sabha. 4. It originates from the Lok Sabha. Select the correct answer from the code given below: Code:
explanation: A Bill is deemed to be a Money Bill when it relates to all or some of the following subjects- • Imposition, Abolition, Avoidance and Alteration of any Tax. Whether a bill is a money bill or not, is the decision of the Speaker of the Lok Sabha will be final. Money bill (Art-110) will not be introduced in Rajya Sabha. After the Money Bill is passed by the Lok Sabha, it will be sent to the Rajya Sabha along with its recommendations (along with it will be a certificate of the Speaker that it is a Money Bill). The Rajya Sabha can neither reject the Money Bill nor make any Amendments to it. It shall return the Bill to Lok Sabha within a period of 14 days from the date of receipt of the Bill. The Lok Sabha will either accept or reject all or any of the recommendations of the Rajya Sabha.

question: A resolution for removing the Vice-President of India can be moved in
explanation: The Vice-President can be removed from his post by a resolution of the Rajya Sabha, which is passed by an effective majority of all members of the Rajya Sabha, with the Lok Sabha also giving its consent. It is necessary to give notice of 14 days before the resolution is proposed.

question: Maximum strength of Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha respectively is
explanation: At present the number of members of Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha are 545 and 250 respectively. The maximum number of members of Rajya Sabha can be 250 (Article.80, structure of Rajya Sabha) while the maximum number of members of Lok Sabha can be

question: As on 2013 the number of Lok Sabha members were 545 but currently the number of Lok Sabha members is 543. 460. The Speaker can ask a member of the House to stop speaking and let another member speak. This is called
explanation: The Speaker can ask a member of the House to stop speaking and let another member speak this is called as Yielding the floor. • Decorum is maintaining proper and polite behavior. • Interpellation–A right of house members to ask question to the government on matters of public interest. • Crossing the floor – A member of parliamentary party who votes against his party in a division is said to be crossing the floor.

question: Who is the ultimate authority to decide, whether a Bill is Financial Bill or not?
explanation: The Speaker of Lok Sabha certifies whether a bill is financial bill or not. Financial bill can be introduced only in Lok Sabha while Rajya Sabha can recommend Amendments in the bill.

question: The Speaker of the Lok Sabha expires hold office till the :

options.b: Term of the Lok Sabha expires
options.c: Next Council of Ministers is constituted
options.d: Next Lok Sabha is constituted Polity

explanation: The Speaker holds office from the date of his/her election till immediately before the first meeting of the Lok Sabha after the general election (for 5 year). • Whenever the Lok Sabha is dissolved, the Speaker does not vacate his office and continues till the newly-elected Lok Sabha meets. • Article 93 of the Constitution provides for the election of both the Speaker and the Deputy Speaker. • The Speaker is the Constitutional and ceremonial head of the House.

question: How many members are to be present in Lok Sabha to make complete the quorum? 1
explanation: A quorum is the minimum number of members required to be present before a meeting is allowed to begin. (cid:1) Article 100 provides that the minimum number of members that must be present to hold the meeting of the Lok Sabha is, either House shall be one-tenth of the total members of the House. (cid:1) If the quorum is not complete it is the duty of the Speaker to adjourn the House or to suspend the meeting until there is quorum.

question: M.P. Jaya Bachchan was disqualified as a member of Rajya Sabha on the ground that she held an office of profit as Chair-person of

options.a: Uttar Pradesh Industrial Development C ouncil

explanation: In the year 2006, MP Jaya Bachchan was disqualified for membership of the Rajya Sabha, as she hold the post of Chairperson in Uttar Pradesh Film Development Council. Currently Jaya Bachchan is a Rajya Sabha MP from Uttar Pradesh from the Samajwadi Party.

question: If any question arises whether a Bill is a Money Bill or not, whose decision out of the following shall be final?

options.a: Decision of the Speaker of the Lok Sabha

explanation: Money Bill is discussed under Article 110. This includes the corollary of any subject mentioned from subsections A to F of Article 110. Despite any bill related to this subject, a Money Bill is not sure until it is declared a money bill by the Chairman. If there is no Money Bill of any bill, the power of final decision rests with the Speaker of Lok Sabha. 239 / 896

question: The Lok Sabha Secretariat comes under the direct supervision of the
explanation: Lok Sabha Secretariat is heeded by Lok Sabha Secretary and it comes under the direct control of Lok Sabha Speaker.

question: Number of MPs in Lok Sabha is
explanation: Maximum Strength of the House is 552 members, 530 members to represent the states and 20 members represent the Union Territories, and 2 members to be nominated by the President from the Anglo-Indian community. At present, the strength of the House is 543. (cid:1) By 104th Amendment Act of 2019, the provision for nomination of 2 Anglo Indian Community Members were repealed in Lok Sabha.

question: In term of the number of electorate the smallest Lok Sabha constituency in India is
explanation: Lakshadweep Lok Sabha constituency is the only Lok Sabha constituency, which covers the entire area of the Union Territory of Lakshadweep in India. It is the smallest Lok Sabha constituency by number of voters.

question: How many members are there in the 'Committee of Privileges' of the Lok Sabha?
explanation: Parliamentary Privileges are certain rights and immunities enjoyed by members of Parliament, individually and collectively, so that they can effectively discharge their functions. Polity Privilege committee– (i) Lok Sabha–The speaker nominates a committee of privileges consisting of 15 members as per respective party strengths. (ii) Rajya Sabha–The deputy chairperson heads the committee of Privileges, that consists of 10 members.

question: Which of the following bodies is/are presided over by a non-member?

options.c: Both Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha

explanation: Rajya Sabha is the permanent house and is presided over by the Vice President of India, who is its ex-officio chairman. He is not a member of Rajya Sabha. A Deputy Chairman is chosen from amongst the members of Rajya Sabha. Rajya Sabha cannot be dissolved.

question: The Parliament of India consists of :

options.a: The President, the Lok Sabha and the Rajya Sabha
options.b: The President, the Vice President, the Lok Sabha and the Rajya Sabha only
options.c: The Lok Sabha and the Rajya Sabha only
options.d: The Union Cabinet, the Lok Sabha and the Rajya Sabha

explanation: The Parliament of India consists of two houses– The Rajya Sabha (Council of States) and the Lok Sabha (House of the people) with the President of India acting as their head. Any bill passed by houses shall not become law until President gives his/her assent to it.

question: The first and the only politician from North- East elected as Speaker of Lok Sabha :
explanation: Purno Agitok Sangma (1 September 1947 March 4, 2016) was an Indian politician who served as the speaker of the Lok-Sabha from 1996 to 1998 and Chief Minister of Meghalaya from 1988 to 1990. Hiteswar Saikia was the chief minister of Assam for two terms first from 28 February 1983 to 23 December 1985 and then from 30 June 1991 to 22 April 1996.

question: Number of Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha seats for Himachal Pradesh are
explanation: The number of Lok Sabha seats are 4 and Rajya Sabha seats are 3 for the state of Himachal Pradesh.

question: In the Union Government, the Council of Ministers is collectively responsible to :

options.c: Both Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha

explanation: In the Union Government, the Council of Ministers is collectively responsible to 'Lok Sabha'. Lok Sabha is also known as "the House of the People". The Lok Sabha is the lower house of the Parliament of India.

question: If the annual Union Budget is not passed by Lok Sabha :

options.a: The President of India asks the Finance Minister to resign
options.b: The Prime Minister asks the Finance Minister to resign
options.c: The Finance Minister modifies it and presents it again
options.d: The Prime Minister submits the resignation of his Cabinet

explanation: If the annual Union Budget is not passed by the Lok Sabha, then the Prime Minister submits the resignation of Council of Ministers. If budget does not pass in any condition in the Parliament, then it is assumed that the rulling party is in a minority, that means the government has lost its vote of confidence in the Lok Sabha and the govt. must resign.

question: 'Lokpal Bill' was introduced in the Lok Sabha for the first time in the year
explanation: The idea of an Anti-Corruption body and an ombudsman to look into corruption allegations against administrators, including legislators has been floated around five decades now. It finally got shape with the passing of the Lokpal and Lokayuktas Bill 2013 in Lok Sabha, on December 18, 2003. • The Lokpal Bill was introduced in Parliament in 1968 but was not passed. • The Bill was passed by Parliament in December 2016 and amended the Lokpal and Lokayukta Act 2013. • First Chairperson of the Lokpal is Shri Justice Pinaki Chandra Ghose.

question: Disqualification of the member of State Legislative Assembly is decided by
explanation: The decision on Disqualification question on the ground of defection is referred to the Speaker or the chairman of the house, and his/her decision is final. Such a decision by the Speaker is subject to judicial as well as a Supreme Court's decision in 1992. Polity

question: How many women were elected to Lok Sabha in the First General Elections?
explanation: In the first Lok Sabha formed in 1952 there were 24 women. The number did not change in the second Lok Sabha formed in 1957.

question: One feature distinguishing the Rajya Sabha from the Legislative Council is
explanation: The President may be removed before his tenure through impeachment for violating the Constitution of India by the Parliament. The process may start in either of the two houses of the parliament. Rajya Sabha distinguished from Legislative Council because in both of them only Rajya Sabha had power of impeachment.

It is a statutory post defined in the salaries and allowances of leaders of opposition in Parliament Act, 1977. Leader of opposition is referred to as the shadow Prime Minister.

explanation: In 2015, the Deputy Chairman of Rajya Sabha was – Prof. P.J. Kurian. • The present Deputy Chairman of Rajya Sabha is Harivansh Narayan Singh (Since 14th September 2020). • Present Lok Sabha Speaker – Om Birla

question: Consider the following statement. I. In case of dispute, over a bill the Constitution provides for a Joint Sitting of the Lok Sabha and the Rajya Sabha for solving the dispute. II. There is no Constitutional provision like Joint Sitting of the State Assembly and the State Council if there is a dispute between them over a bill.

options.b: Statement I is correct but statement II is incorrect
options.c: Statement I is incorrect but statement II is correct
options.d: Both the statements are incorrect Polity

explanation: • In case of dispute, over a bill the joint sitting of the Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha is called by the President of India (Article 108) for solving the dispute. • There is no Constitutional provision like joint sitting of the state assembly and the state council if there is a dispute between them over a bill.

question: In which election Congress party won maximum seats in Lok Sabha?
explanation: In the Lok Sabha Elections of 1984 the Congress Party came to power winning 414 of the 514 Lok Sabha seats more than 80% of the seats. • INC was founded in 1885 • Womesh Chandra Bonerjee was the first President of INC.

question: When is the Rajya Sabha dissolved?
explanation: Rajya Sabha is permanent body, it is never dissolved. Each member of the Rajya Sabha serves for a term of six year, but one third of its member retire at the expiration of every two year.

question: What is the maximum strength of Lok Sabha?
explanation: The maximum strength of Lok Sabha was 545 as on 2006. Article 79–The Council of the Parliament of the Union. Article 81– Composition of the House of People or Lok Sabha. At present the maximum strength of the house is 552 members in which 530 members to represent the states, 20 members to represent the union territories and 2 members to be nominated by the President from the Anglo Indian Community. Note :- By the 104 Amendment Act 2020, The Angle India seat in Lok Sabha has been removed.

question: What was the name of that President who had also served as speaker of Lok Sabha?
explanation: Neelam Sanjeev Reddy was elected to Parliament in 1977 as a candidate of the Janta Party. He was unanimously elected speaker of Lok Sabha and three months later was elected unopposed as sixth President of India (1977-1982). Therefore he had served as President as well as speaker of Lok Sabha.

question: Who is the Chairman of Lok Sabha?
explanation: The Speaker is the chairperson of Lok Sabha. The speaker elected generally in the first meeting of the Lok Sabha following general elections to serve for five years, chosen from sitting members of the Lok Sabha. 242 / 896

question: Which one of the following sets of Bills is presented to the Parliament along with the budget?

options.a: Direct taxes Bill and Indirect taxes Bill

explanation: Finance Bill and Appropriation Bill is presented to the parliament along with the budget. • A Finance Bill is a Money Bill as defined in Article 110 of the Constitution. It can be introduced only in Lok Sabha. • Appropriation Bill (also known as supply bill or spending bill) is a proposed law that authorizes the expenditure of government funds.

question: The Council of Ministers under Article 75(3) is

options.a: Collectively responsible to the House of the people
options.b: Collectively responsible to the Council of States
options.c: Collectively responsible to the President of India
options.d: Collectively responsible to the House of the people as well as the council of states

explanation: India has parliamentary form of government and collective responsibility is one of the important features of this system. As per provision of Article 75(3), Council of Ministers are collectively responsible to Lok Sabha or House of the people.

question: Which of the following statements is/are correct regarding Zero Hour? 1. The term 'Zero Hour' is not formally recognized in our parliamentary procedure. 2. There is no maximum limit on the number of matters that can be raised on any given day. 3. Parliamentary Affair Minister is the final authority to allow or not allow the raising of matters in the House under Zero Hour.
explanation: Zero hour the time immediately following the question hour has come to be known as "Zero Hour". It starts at around 12 noon and members can with prior notice to the speaker, raise issues of importance during this time. 'Zero hour' is not mentioned in the rules of procedure. In zero hour there is no maximum limit on the number of matters that can be raised on any given day.

question: Though the Council of Ministers is collectively responsible to the Lok Sabha, the individual Ministers are Constitutionally responsible to .......
explanation: Though the Council of Ministers is collectively responsible to the Lok Sabha, the individual Ministers are Constitutionally responsible to the President. Article 75 in the Constitution of India (1) The Prime Minister shall be appointed by the President and the other Minister shall be appointed by the President on the advice of the Prime Minister. (2) The Minister shall hold office during the pleasure of the President.

question: Who was the first Chairman of the States Reorganization Commission?

The first Chairman of state reorganization commission was retired Chief Justice of the Supreme Court Fazal Ali. The State Reorganization Act (1956) and 7th Constitutional Amendment Act (1956), accepted these recommendations with certain minor modifications. explanation: Zonal Councils are advisory councils and are made up of the states of India that have been grouped into five zones to foster cooperation among them. These were setup under part-III of the State Reroganisation Act 1956.

question: The Right to Education was inserted by which Constitution Amendment Act?
explanation: The 86th Constitutional Amendment Act 2002 inserted Article 21-A in the Constitution of India to provide free and compulsory education of all children in the age group of 6–14 years as a Fundamental Right in such a manner as the State may by law determine.

question: What is the majority required in Parliament to change the name of a state?

(cid:1) The sexual harassment of women at workplace (Prevention, Prohibition and Redressal) Act, 2013 is a legislative Act in India that seeks protect women from sexual harassment at their places of work. It was passed by the Lok Sabha on 3 September 2012 and by the Rajya Sabha on 26 Feb 2013.

question: How many Land Reforms Acts were included in the Ninth Schedule by the 78th Amendment Act?

question: According to the recently Panel Central Act what can be the maximum number of Ministers in the Central Cabinet?

options.a: 10% of the authorized strength of Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha
options.b: 15% of the authorized strength of Lok Sabha Polity

explanation: Article 72 of the Constitution prescribes that the total number of Ministers including the Prime Minister, in the Council of Ministers shall not exceed 15% of the number of members of the House of the People. The Amendment 91st in 2003 added clause (1A) in this Article which made a specific provision that the total number of Ministers, including Prime Minister in no case exceed 15% of the total number of Lok Sabha Members.

question: Which one of the following Amendments of the Indian Constitution restricts the size of the ministry?
explanation: The size of the Cabinet was curbed by the 91st Constitution Amendment Act of the Indian Constitution. These act provided that the total number of Ministers including the Prime Minister in the Council of Ministers should not exceed 15% of the total number of Members of the House.

question: The provision that the total number of in the Council of Ministers shall not exceed 15% of the total number of members of the house of the people has been made by :
explanation: The total number of Ministers, including the Prime Minister of the Council of Ministers, shall not exceed fifteen percent of the total number of members of the Lok Sabha. It is under the Indian Constitution Amendment 91st.

question: Under which Article of the Constitution is the Governor of a State required to constitute a state Finance Commission every five years to decide resource allocation between state government and Panchayati Raj Institutions?

question: Which of the following Constitutional authority can hold office during the pleasure of the President?

The Governor may, by writing under his hand addressed to the President, resign his office. Expiration of his term, continuous to hold office until his successor enters upon his office.

question: Who was appointed as the Governor of Odisha by President Ram Nath Kovind in May 2018?
explanation: Prof. Ganeshi Lal was appointed as the Governor of Odhisha by the President Ramnath Kovind 29th on May 2018.

question: Who among the following can be removed from the office without impeachment?
explanation: The Governor holds office during the pleasure of the President. The President can remove the Governor before the end of his term or can extend his term for another five year. The Governor can resign from his post but no impeachment proceedings against him are contemplated in the Constitution. Note- Chief Election Commissioner can be removed by the President on the basis of resolution passed by both the houses of parliament with a two-third majority.

question: Which one of the following statements is correct?

options.a: In India, the same person cannot be appointed as Governor for two or more States at the same time

options.c: No procedure has been laid down in the Constitution of India for the removal of a Governor from his post
options.d: In the case of a Union Territory having a legislative set up, the Chief Minister is appointed by the Lt. Governor

explanation: No procedure has been laid down in the Constitution of India for the removal of a Governor from his/her post. Dismissal by the President (usually on the advice of the Prime Minister of the country) at whose pleasure the governor holds office. Dismissal of Governors without valid reason is not permitted at all. It is the duty of the President to dismiss a governor whose acts are upheld by courts as unConstitutional and malafide.

question: Consider the following statements : 1. Governor of a State can reserve certain bills passed by the State Legislature for consideration of the President of India 2. Governor of a State can make the rules to conduct the business of State Government Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
explanation: Governor of a State can reserve certain bills passed by the State Legislature for consideration of the President. When a bill other than money bill is presented before Governor for his assent, the governor either gives assent to the bill, wit hold his assent to the bill, return the bill for reconsideration of houses but if the bill is passed again by State Legislature with or without Amendments, he has to give his assent or reserve the bill for consideration of President. (Article 200). Under Article 166, he can make the rules to conduct the business of State Government.

question: Assertion (A) : Under the Constitution, the Chief Minister holds office till the pleasure of the Governor. Reason (R) : The Chief Minister is appointed by the Governor.

options.a: Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).
options.b: Both (A) and (R) are true but (R) is not a correct explanation of (A).
options.d: (A) is false but (R) is true. Polity

explanation: As Per Article 164 (1) Chief Minister shall be appointed by the Governor and other Ministers shall be appointed by the Governor on the advise of Chief Minister. Chief Minister and other Ministers holds office during the pleasure of the Governor. Hence Both A & R are correct and R is the correct explanation of A.

question: The Governor is justified in recommending President's Rule only

options.a: in anticipation of horse-trading in the house
options.b: in anticipation of loss of majority
options.c: when the ruling party fails the floor test in the Assembly and there is no alternative
options.d: on the ground of an unstable government

explanation: In India, President's Rule is the suspension of State government and imposition of direct Union government rule in a state under Article 356 of the Indian Constitution. In the event that a State government is unable to function according to Constitutional provision, the union government can take, direct control of the State machinery. President's rule can be imposed in a state under anyone of the following circumstances : (1) A state legislature is unable to elect a leader as Chief Minister for a time prescribed by the Governor of that state at the will of Governor. (2) Loss of majority in the assembly due to a vote of no. confidence in the house. (3) State's Constitutional machinery or legislature fails to abide by Constitutional norms.

question: Who among the following is the sole representative of the state government in the district?

options.b: Member of parliament from the District

explanation: The District Collector is the highest officer of Revenue Administration in the district. In Revenue matters, he/she is responsible to the Government through the Divisional Commissioner and Financial Commissioner.

question: Who amongst the following holds the office during the pleasure of President?

question: When can the Governor issue an ordinance?

options.a: When the Union Government asks him to do so.
options.b: Whenever there is a law and order problem in the state.
options.c: Whenever State Legislature is not in session.
options.d: Whenever the judiciary advises him to do so.

explanation: The Governor of a state can issue ordinances under Article 213 of the Constitution, when the state legislative assembly (or either of the two houses in states with bicameral legislatures) is not in session. Article 123 deals with ordinance making powers of President.

question: The term of office of the Governor of a State is

options.a: Shall not be a member of either houses of
options.b: Should not be above 35 years of age
options.c: Should not be an Indian citizen
options.d: Should also hold any other office of profit

explanation: The Governor of a state shall be appointed by the President. The Governor of a state shall not be a member of either house of Parliament or any state legislature. A person to be eligible for appointment as governor should be citizen of India and has completed the age of 35 years (Article 157).

question: Governor of a State is appointed by the President on the advice of ......
explanation: Article 156 of the Indian Constitution states that President of India appoints the Governor of the State for a period of 5 years on the adivce of the Prime Minister of India. Article 153 – There shall be a governor for each state. The Governor of a State shall be appointed by the President by warrant under his hand and seal (Article 155). Article 157 – A person to be eligible for appointment as Governor should be citizen of India and has completed age of 35 years.

question: The executive power of the state is vested in :
explanation: The executive power of the state is vested in the Governor and Shall be exercised by him either directly or through officers subordinate to him in accordance with the Constitution of India under Article 154. Polity

question: There is no provision for the impeachment of

options.b: The Chief Justice of the High Court

explanation: There is no provision for the impeachment or removal of the Governor of state. Note :– (1) Impeachment word is related to Article-61 for the President of India. (2) The process of removing of the Chief Justice of Supreme Court and High Court is same as impeachment of the President, but the word impeachment is not used in the process.

question: Which of statement is not correct regarding motion for adjournment on a matter of public importance in the Rajasthan Legislative Assembly?

options.a: Notice of an adjournment motion shall be given before the commencement of the sitting on the day on which the motion is proposed to be made to the Speaker
options.b: The motion shall be restricted to a specific matter of recent occurrence
options.c: The motion shall not raise a question of privilege
options.d: Not more than one such motion shall be made at the same sitting

explanation: The main object of an adjournment motion is to draw the attention of the house to a recent matter of urgent public importance having serious consequences and with regard to which motion or a resolution with proper notice will be too late.

question: Who among the following has sworn in as the Chief Minister for highest number of times in Rajasthan?
explanation: Mohan Lal Sukhadia became the Chief Minister when he was 38 years of age and was accountable for bringing key developments and reforms in Rajasthan. He is usually admired as the "Founder of modern Rajasthan". Mohan Lal Sukhadia has sworn in as the chief minister for highest (4 times) number of times in Rajasthan.

question: If there is a notice of a Motion expressing No- confidence in Council of Ministers in Rajasthan Assembly and at the same time a motion expressing confidence in the Council of Ministers is received, then which motion shall get precedence?
explanation: A motion of confidence is a motion of support proposed by a government in a parliament. Government often proposes a motion of confidence to replace a motion of no confidence proposed by opposition. No Confidence motion– A no-confidence motion can be moved by any member of the House. It can be moved only in the Lok Sabha and not Rajya Sabha.

question: In which year, Sikkim became a State of India?
explanation: On 16 May 1975, Sikkim became the 22nd state of the Indian Union.

question: What is the minimum number of Members required for the composition of a State Legislative Assembly as per the Constitution of India?
explanation: The Constitution of India states that the Legislative Assembly must have not less than 60 and no more than 500 members. However an exception may be granted via an Act of Parliament as is the case in the states of Goa, Sikkim, Mizoram and the Union Territory of Puducherry which have less than 60 members.

question: The total number of ministers, including the Chief Minister, in the Council of Ministers in a state:

options.a: Cannot be more than 21% of the total number of members of the Legislative Assembly of that state
options.c: Cannot be more than 15% of the total number of members of the Legislative Assembly of that state

explanation: The maximum number of ministers, including the Chief Minister, in the Union Council of Minister shall not exceed 15% of the total number of members of the Legislative Assembly but the minimum number of ministers including the Chief Minister of a state shall not be less than a minimum of 12 members.

question: No money bill can be introduced in an assembly without the permission of the -------.
explanation: No money bill can be introduced in the Legislative Assembly, except on the recommendation of the Governor. • In the Center, Money Bills can be introduced only in Lok Sabha with the prior approval of the President. • The speaker of the Lok Sabha decides a bill is a money bill or not. • Article 110 deals with Money Bill.

question: Which one of the following States has granted Sanskrit language the status of the second official language of the State? Polity
explanation: The Uttarakhand government gave Sanskrit the status of second official language of the state. This comes after Chief Minister Ramesh Pokhariyal said that he intended to promote Sanskrit in the state. In fact, Uttarakhand is the only state to have accorded Sanskrit this status.

question: Who among the following, appointed by the Governor, can be removed by the President of India?

options.a: The Chief Minister of a State
options.b: A Member of the State Public Service Commission
options.d: The Vice-Chancellor of a State University

explanation: The Governor appoints the Chairman and Members of State Public Service Commission but they can only be removed by the President. Advocate General is the Chief Law Officer and legal Advisor of State Government. He is appointed by the Governor and holds office during the pleasure of Governor.

question: Read the given Assertion and Reason, and select the most appropriate option with respect to them. Assertion: A minister at the state level continues in office till he enjoys the confidence of the chief minister. Reason: The chief minister can ask him to resign or advise the Governor to dismiss him in case of difference of opinion.

options.a: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
options.b: Both A and R are true but R is not a correct explanation of A.
options.c: A is true but R is false.
options.d: A is false but R is true.

explanation: A Minister of the State is appointed by the Governor on the advice of Chief Minister. He continues in office till he enjoys the confidence of the CM. At any time the Chief Minister can ask him to resign or advise the Governor to dismiss him. Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.

question: Which of the following states has bicameral legislature?
explanation: Bicameral legislature consists of two layers of assemblies or chambers. The following Indian States have a bicameral legislature : (i) Uttar Pradesh (ii) Maharashtra (iii) Bihar (iv) Karnataka (v) Andhra Pradesh (vi) Telangana.

question: What is the most important function of the legislature?
explanation: The lower house of the Indian Parliament in the state legislative is known as the State Legislative Assembly or the Vidhan Sabha. The main function of such an assembly is to formulate laws and regulation.

question: One-third members of the Council of States retire
explanation: One third of the members of State Legislative Council retire after every two years. The tenure of MLCs is Six Years.

question: Governor will act on the advice of council of Ministers while

options.b: Appointing the chairman of the stale public service commission
options.c: Recommending for President’s rule in the state

explanation: • Governor will act on the advice of the Council of Ministers while dissolving the Legislative Assembly. • The Council of Ministers is collectively responsible to the Legislative Assembly of the State and may be removed from office by adverse voting therein. • The Council of Ministers to aid and advise Governor conists in Article 163. • Governor can rightly claim that dissolution comes within such wide authority.

question: Which of the following State is not having bicameral legislature?
explanation: At present, only Six States have a bicameral legislature they are Uttar Pradesh, Andhra Pradesh, Karnataka, Telangana, Maharashtra and Bihar. This means that twenty two States have a Unicameral legislature. Tamil Nadu state have not a bicameral legislature.

question: Maximum possible members of state Vidhan Sabha is
explanation: The Legislative Assembly consists of not more than 500 Members and not less than 60. The biggest state like Uttar Pradesh has 403 members in its Assembly. States which have Small population and are small in size have a provision for having even lesser number of members in the Legislative Assembly. All members of the Legislative Assembly are elected on the basis of Adult Franchise.

question: Which one of the following cannot be dissolved but can be abolished ?
explanation: State Legislative Council or Rajya Sabha is a permanent body and is not subject to dissolution but can be abolish. • A member is elected to the Rajya Sabha for a full term period of six year. • One-third of its members retire every second year.

question: The Legislative Council in a state can be abolished by the Parliament on the recommendation of-
explanation: The Legislative Council of a state can be created and abolished by the Parliament on the recommendation of concerned State Legislative Assembly.

question: What can be maximum number of persons authorized in a State Cabinet as per act recently passed?

options.a: 10% of the authorized strength of the MLAS and the MLCs.
options.c: 20% of the MLAs and the MLCs on the side of the ruling group.
options.d: 15% of the authorized strength of the MLAs.

explanation: The maximum strength of Council of Ministers in a state as per Constitution is 15% of Legislative Assembly. Amendment of Article 164- (1A). The total number of Ministers, including Chief Minister in the Council of Minister in a State shall not exceed fifteen percent

question: Under which one of the following Articles of Indian Constitution, provisions for creation or abolition of legislative councils in states are made?
explanation: Legislative Council or Vidhan Parishad is the Upper House in bicameral legislatures in some States of India. Article 169 of the Indian Constitution deals with the creation or abolition of State Legislative Council.

question: The number of seats in the Legislative Assembly of NCT of Delhi is :
explanation: The number of seats in the Legislative Assembly of NCT of Delhi is 70. Delhi Vidhan Sabha is a unicameral law making body of National Capital Territory of Delhi.

question: The only Chief Minister in India who continuously ruled for more than five terms :
explanation: Jyoti Basu served as the Chief Minister of West Bengal form 1977 to 2000. Basu was the longest serving Chief Minister in India until Chamling surpassed him in 2018. Pawan Kumar Chamling, the former Chief Minister of Sikkim, holds the distinction of being the longest- serving Chief Minister after Independence. Pawan Kumar Chamling ruled in Sikkim from December 12, 1994 to May 26, 2019.

question: Strength of Assembly of Goa is
explanation: The strength of Legislative Assembly of Goa state is 40. It is a unicameral legislature.

question: The council of Ministers in a state is collectively responsible to
explanation: The Council of Ministers in a State are collectively responsible to the Legislative Assembly of the state. The Council of Ministers in Union are collectively responsible to the Lok Sabha.

question: Of the following States, which has two Houses?
explanation: Andhra Pradesh, Bihar, Karnataka, Maharashtra, Telangana and Uttar Pradesh have bicameral legislatures. In legistetiv council one third of its members are elected every two years.

question: Which of the following are not necessarily the consequences of the proclamation of the President's rule in a State? (1) Dissolution of the State Legislative Assembly (2) Removal of the Council of Ministers in the state (3) Dissolution of local bodies (4) Takeover of the state administration by the union government. Choose the correct answer from the codes given below:
explanation: Under Article 356 President rule is imposed in a state. Dissolution of Local bodies are not the consequence of proclamation of President Rule. Dissolution of State Legislative Assembly is not necessarily the consequences of the proclamation of the President Rule. When the Presidents rule is imposed in a state the President dismisses the state council of minister's headed by the chief minister.

question: Which of the following offices does not exist in the Council of Ministers of Chhattisgarh?
explanation: Deputy Minister Office does not exist in the Council of Ministers of Chhattisgarh.

question: At present, what is the number of the members of the legislative assembly of Chhattisgarh?
explanation: The Vidhan Sabha comprises of 90 member of Legislative Assembly of Chhattisgarh.

question: It was carved out of the Southern portion of Bihar. 646. Consider the following statements. I. State Legislative Councils are continuous bodies. II. Members of State Legislative Councils are indirectly elected. III. The President by an ordinance can dissolve a State Legislative Council. IV. Actual strength of the State Legislative Council depends upon the strength of the State Legislative Assembly.

options.a: Statements I, II and IV are correct
options.b: Statements III and IV are correct

explanation: State legislative councils are continuous bodies. • Members of state legislative council are indirectly elected • Actual strength of the state legislative council depends upon the strength of the state legislative assembly

question: In which state it is compulsory to have a separate Minister for Tribal Welfare?
explanation: Article 164 of the Indian Constitution talks about of having Council of Ministers and a Chief Minister in every state of India. It also states that for the state of Bihar, Madhya Pradesh and Orissa, there shall be a Minister in charge of tribal welfare who may in addition be in charge of the welfare of the Scheduled castes and backward classes or any other works. 261 / 896

question: In which matter 2/3 majority of Legislative Assembly is essential? (A) To form a new state (B) To create a Legislative Council (C) To abolish the Legislative Council (D) To ratify the Amendment bill on Article 368 (E) To ratify the bill amending basic structure of the Constitution
explanation: To create or abolish a state legislative Council. The State Legislative Assembly must pass a resolution, which must be supported by majority of the strength of the house and 2/3rd majority of the present and voting. A number of provisions in the Constitution can be amended by a simple majority of the two houses of the Parliament outside the scope of Article 368. The majority of the provisions in the Constitution need to be amended by a special majority of the Parliament. The Provision of creating new states and altering the boundary of a state is given in Article 3 of the Indian Constitution. A Simple majority of Parliament is required for creation of new state.

question: The number of members of State Legislative Assembly cannot be more than
explanation: The Constitution of India states that a State Legislative Assembly must have not less than 60 and not more than 500 members however an exception may be granted via an Act of Parliament as is the case in the states of Goa, Sikkim, Mizoram and the Union Territory of Puducherry which have fewer than 60 members. A State Legislative Assembly may be dissolved in the state of emergency by the governor on request of the Chief Minister or if a motion of no confidence is passed against the ruling majority party or coalition.

question: Among the following, which state has the highest Lok Sabha Seats?
explanation: Lok Sabha is composed of representative of people chosen by direct election on the basis of adult suffrage. • The total seat allotted by Constitution is 552 seat. (i) Election made only upto 530 seat. (ii) Upto 20 member to represent the union territory. Uttar Pradesh has the highest Lok Sabha seats. It has 80 seats in Lok Sabha. Madhya Pradesh has 29 Lok Sabha seats. Rajasthan has 25 Lok Sabha seats. Andhra Pradesh after bifurcation has 25 seats in Lok Sabha.

question: What is a law called while it is being made in the Legislature?
explanation: Any law which is being made in the legislature is called "Bill" when it passed from both the houses. It goes to governor for his consent after his consent it is called an "Act". Policy– Policies are rules, principles, guidelines or frame works that are adopted or designed by an organization to achieve long term goals. White Paper– White papers are policy documents produced by the Government that set out their proposals for future legislation. Blue Print– A blueprint is a paper which is blue with plans for a building printed on it. Resolution– The act of finding an answer of solution to a conflict, problem etc.

question: The Legislative Council has been abandoned in the following states

options.a: Andhra Pradesh, Tamil Nadu and Punjab
options.b: Andhra Pradesh, Maharashtra and West Bengal
options.c: Andhra Pradesh, Tamil Nadu, Bihar and Maharashtra

explanation: The Tamil Nadu Legislative council (Abolition) Bill, 1986 was passed by both houses of the parliament and received the assent of the President on 30 August 1986. (cid:1) The Punjab Legislative council (Abolition) Act, 1969 was a legislation in India, adopted in 1969. Through this legislation the Punjab Legislative council was abolished. (cid:1) In the 1980s, Andhra Pradesh became one of the first states to seek the abolition of the upper houses, which were being increasingly criticized as being unnecessary, unrepresentative of the population, a burden on the state budget and causing delays in passing legislation.

question: State Provisions for some states 653. Which one of the following states has a separate Constitution?

options.d: :- It states that other Constitutional provisions may be applied from time to time with some modifications made by President of India. Note :- The special status of Jammu and Kashmir was abolished on 6 August, 2019. 262 / 896

explanation: Jammu and Kashmir state had a separate Constitution before August 6, 2019. Article 370 of the Constitution of India was a "temporary provision" that granted the state of Jammu and Kashmir a special autonomous status within the Indian Union.

question: Which among the following is not listed in the 6th Schedule of the Constitution of India?
explanation: Sixth Schedule in the Indian Constitution established Autonomous District Councils (ADC) in four northeastern states, namely Assam, Meghalaya, Tripura and Mizoram. These ADC envisage protecting and preserving tribal culture.

question: Article 370 of the India Constitution relates to:

options.a: Temporary provisions with respect to the state of Jammu and Kashmir
options.d: The representation of states in the Parliament

explanation: Article 370 of the India Constitution gave special status to Jammu and Kashmir. On 5 August 2019, the Government of India revoked the special status granted under Article 370 of the Indian Constitution to Jammu and Kashmir.

question: The Supreme Court of India was established in Calcutta through which of the following Acts?
explanation: The Regulating Act of 1773 provided for the establishment of a Supreme Court at Calcutta in year 1774 comprising of one Chief Justice and three other Judges. Sir Elijah Imphey was appointed as cheif Justice of this court. It had power to try civil, criminal, admirarlty cases and it had to be a court of record.

question: Which of the following are included in the original jurisdiction of the Supreme Court? 1. A dispute between the Government of India and one or more states 2. A dispute regarding elections to either House of Parliament or that of Legislature of a state 3. A dispute between the Government of India and a Union Territory 4. A dispute between two or more states
explanation: In India, the Supreme Court has Original, Appellants and Advisory Jurisdiction. Its exclusive original jurisdiction extends to all cases between the government of India and the states of India or between Government of India and states on one side and one or more states on other side or cases between different states.

question: Which of the following Articles is associated with the establishment and Constitution of Supreme Court?
explanation: Article 124 is associated with the establishment and Constitution of Supreme Court. It established the Supreme Court of India and also laid out provisions relating to the appointment, impeachment and conduct of its judges.

question: The first female judge of the Supreme Court of India is
explanation: Justice Fatima Bibi was the first female judge of Supreme Court. She was appointed in 1989, 40 years after the establishment of Supreme Court. Anna Chandy was the first female judge of High Court in India. She was known for the first Woman judge in commonwealth countries. Indu Malhotra is a retired judge and senior counsel of the Supreme Court. Indira Banerjee was appointed as the 8th female judge in history of Supreme Court of India. She is former Chief Justice of Madras High Court.

question: The present Chief Justice of the Supreme Court is
explanation: When question was asked Chief Justice of India was Ranjan Gogoi. Justice SA Bobde the senior most judge in the supreme court after "CJI Ranjan Gogoi (In 2019)". • 48th Currently N.V. Ramana is Chief Justice of the Supreme Court.

question: With reference to the Constitutional provisions, which of the following statements about the Supreme Court is/are true? (1) It is a court of record (2) It is NOT the highest court of appeal in India. (3) It has the power to punish for contempt of itself.

options.e: None of the statement is correct. 263 / 896

explanation: The Supreme Court of India is the apex judicial body of India. Article 129 of the Constitution of India which makes the Supreme Court shall be a court of record and shall have all the powers of such a court including the power to punish contempt of itself. Article 142 which allows the court to provide punishment for contempt. The Supreme Court is the highest court of appeal in India.

question: As per the Constitution of India, who is authorized to transfer the Judge of the High Court to another High Court?

options.b: The Speaker of the Lok Sabha

explanation: The President may, after consultation with Chief Justice of India, transfer a judge from one High Court to another High Court.

question: Which Article provides for the appointment of ad-hoc judges in the Supreme Court?
explanation: Article 127 of Indian Constitution provides for the appointment of ad-hoc judges in the Supreme Court. It states that the persons who are qualified to be appointed as judges of the Supreme Court can be appointed as ad hoc judges of the Supreme Court. Note :- Ad-hoc judges can be appointed in the Supreme Court by the “chief Justice of India” with the prior consent of the President if there is no quorum of judges available to hold and continue the session of the court.

question: In which one of the following courts, there is provision for appointment of adhoc judges?
explanation: Article 127 of the Indian Constitution provides for the appointment of an adhoc judge. According to this, if at any time the quorum of the judges of that court is not complete for holding or running the session of the Supreme Court, then the Chief Justice of India, with the prior consent of the President and after consulting the Chief Justice of the High Court concerned, a judge of the court, who holds the necessary qualifications for appointment of a Judge, may be appointed as Adhoc Judge for the necessary period in the court meetings. The Judge so appointed shall have all the powers, privileges and jurisdiction of a Judge of the Supreme Court so long as he has to act as a Judge of the Supreme Court.

question: The Supreme Court of India was set up by the
explanation: • The Regulating Act of 1773 established the Supreme Court of Judicature at Fort William in Calcutta. • Pitt's India Act, 1784 resulted in dual control or joint government in India by Crown in Great Britain and the British East India Company, with crown having ultimate authority. • Charter Act of 1813 asserted the Crown's sovereignty over British India by defining the Constitutional position of British in India. • Charter Act 1833 was intended to provide for an extension of the royal charter granted to the East India Company.

question: The writ that books into the authority of a person in performing an official act is
explanation: 'Mandamus' means 'we command'. It is issued by the Supreme Court or High Court to lower court or direct a public authority to perform the legal duties or statutory duty which it has not or refused to perform. It can be issued by the court against a public official, public, corporation, tribunal and inferior court of the government.

question: Which officer of the Government of India has the right to take part in the proceedings of parliament of India even though he is not a member?

He has the right of audience in all Courts in the Territory of India and has the right to speak and to take part in the proceedings of both the houses of Parliament or their joint sitting and any Committee of the Parliament of which he may be named a member, but without a right to vote.

explanation: Every Zilla Parishad shall hold meetings at least once in every three months, at such time and at such place within the local limits of the district concerned as the Zilla Parishad may fix at the immediately preceding meeting.

question: Which of the following State does not have Panchayati Raj Institution at all?
explanation: Part-IX of the Constitution of India deals with the Panchayati Raj System for the rural areas. It consists of definitions of various terms, composition, duration, reservation, powers etc. of a Panchayat. question: The Gram Sabha constitutes the in the Panchayati Raj system.
explanation: A three-tier structure of the Indian administration for rural development is called Panchayati Raj. The aim of the Panchayati Raj is to develop local self-governments in districts, zones and villages. In India Panchayati Raj now functions as a system of governance in which Gram Panchayats are the basic units of local administration. The system has three levels: Gram Panchayat (village level), Mandal Parishad or Block Samiti or Panchayat Samiti (block level), and Zila Parishad (district level).

question: Which subjects(s) falls/fall within the jurisdiction of Panchayats?

An urban centre with more than 11,000 and less than 25,000 inhabitants in classified as a Nagar Panchayat. For the creation of Nagar Panchayats the density of the population therein, the revenue generated for local administration and economic importance of the area are taken into consideration. question: The need for Constitutional sanction to village panchayats was first felt by the:

question: Panchayat Samiti at the block level in India is a/an ………….
explanation: In India the Panchayati Raj System has three levels – Gram Panchayat at village level, Panchayat Samiti at Block level and Zila Parishad at District level. question: Which of the following body recommended that the main executive organ of the Panchayati Raj System should be located at the district level in the form of “Zila Parishad”
explanation: Ashok Mehta Committee on Panchayati Raj was appointed in 1977. The committee recommendation was 'Zila Parishad should be the executive body and made responsible for planning at the district level. • Ashok Mehta committee recommended a two tier system instead of a three tier system (Zila parishad at the district level, and below it, the Mandal Panchayat.) 275 / 896

question: Under which Article of the Indian Constitution is the District Planning Commission constituted?
explanation: The ninth chapter Panchayati Raj has been 73rd added to the Indian Constitution by the 11th Constitutional Amendment Act. A new Schedule was also added to the Constitution by this Act. Presently, Panchayati Raj system is applicable in all states of the India except Nagaland, Meghalaya, Mizoram States and Union Territory Delhi.

question: ASHA in the context of Panchayat Raj refers to

options.b: Apprentice in Service of Health Advancement

explanation: Accredited Social Health Activist (ASHA) instituted by Ministry of Health and Family Welfare (MoHFW) as a part of India's National Rural Health Minister, is a health activist in the community who will create awareness on health and its social determinants and mobilize the community towards local health planning and increased utilization and accountability of the existing health services. It began in 2005.

question: Article 40 of the Constitution of India states that "The state shall take steps to organize X and endow them with such powers and authority as may be necessary to enable them to function as units of self-government. In statement 'X' stands for
explanation: Article 40 of the Constitution which Enshrines one of the Directive Principles of State Policy lays down that “the state shall take steps to organizes village Panchayats and endow them with such powers and authority as may be necessary to enable them to function as units of self government. Accordingly, a three-tier Panchayati Raj System was 73rd introduced through the Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992. Polity

question: Consider the following statements: 1. Panchayati Raj is a four tier system of governance. 2. Members of Gram Panchayat can hold Lok Adalats. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
explanation: In India, the Panchayati Raj now functions as a system of governance in which Gram Panchayats are basic units of local administration. The system has three tier Gram Panchayat (village level) Mandal Parishad or Block Samiti or Panchayat Samiti (block level), and Zila Parishad (district level). Lok Adalat based on the principles of "Panch Parmeshwar", Lok Adalat derived from a Sanskrit word "Loka" which means people and "Adalat" which means court, Lok Adalat is an innovative contribution in the Indian Judiciary System and it succeeded in providing a supplementary forum to the victims for a satisfactory settlement of their disputes.

question: Under which provision does the governor constitute the State Finance Commission to review the financial status of the Panchayat?

options.a: By the discretion of the Governor
options.b: Through law passed in the Legislative Assembly
options.c: Through Article 243(I) of Constitution of India
options.d: Through the motion passed in Rajya Sabha

explanation: Under Article 243(I) of the Constitution of India, the governor of a state is required to constitute a Finance Commission every five years. This is in order to decide the resource allocation between the state government and the Panchayati Raj Institutions. Article 243(Y) also brought city councils or municipalities under the review of the State Finance Commission.

question: What is NOT included in the jurisdiction of Education Committee of Janpad Panchayat?
explanation: Education Committee of Janpad Panchayat responsible for education including adult education social welfare of the disabled and destitute, woman and child welfare and relief of distress caused by flood, drought earthquake, health and sanitation. Communication is not included.

question: A member of Legislative Assembly, who is ex- officio member of Janpad Panchayat may be nominated as ex-officio member in which committee of Janpad Panchayat?
explanation: A Ex-officio Member of Legislative Assembly may be nominated as ex-officio Member of Education Committee, Agriculture Committee and General Administrative Committee of Janpad Panchayat.

question: In which meeting of Gram Sabha, is the budget of Gram Panchayat being passed?
explanation: All decision of the Panchayat are taken through the Gram Sabha and no decision is official and valid without the consent of Gram Sabha. In an ordinary meeting budget of Gram Panchayat is passed.

options.e: The post Sarpanch will be vacant

explanation: The Sarpanch of the Gram Panchayat shall be elected by the persons registered in the electoral rolls for the territorial constituencies of the Panchayat area from among themselves. If the election of Sarpanch pending due to some reason then a Panch elected by Gram Panchayat Act as a Sarpanch.

question: Who gives the power to the local self- government?
explanation: Lord Ripon is known as Father of Local Self Government in India. The Constitution is the ultimate source of power for the Local self Government. Though the states, through an act can give certain powers to the Panchayats and Municipalities.

question: The members are elected 2. One woman member is included in the committee. 3. One OBC member is included in the committee. 783. If a Gram Panchayat dissolved by a competent authority what will happen ? (A) A committee shall be appointed by the competent authority. (B) The committee will exercise the powers of the Gram Panchayat. 279 / 896 (C) The new Gram Panchayat shall be constituted. (D) The head of the committee shall be nominated by the competent authority. (E) The head of the committee shall be elected by the members of committee.
explanation: In the opinion of Zila Parishad, if a Gram Panchayat exceeds or abuses its powers or is not competent to performance of the duties imposed on it, then Zila Parishad, by an order published in the official Gazette, may dissolve such Gram Panchayat. If a Gram Panchayat gets dissolved by a competent authority then a committee shall be appointed by the competent authority and the head of the committee shall be nominated by the competent authority. This committee will exercise the power of the Gram Panchayat then the new Gram Panchayat shall be constituted by elections.

question: Which one of the following Articles of the Constitution provides that superintendence, direction and control of elections shall be vested in an Election Commission?
explanation: In Article 324 of Indian Constitution, a provision has been made for the Election Commission. This is the apex organization for conducting elections in free and fair manner in India to ensure public participation in elections. This Article vests in the Election Commission the right to superintend, direct and control elections. In this, the election rules, instructions and rights of the President, Vice-President and State Legislature are told.

explanation: Article 315 – As per Article 315 of the Indian Constitution, two or more states may agree that there shall be one Public Service Commission for that group of States. explanation: The Mandal Commission officially known as the socially and educationally Backward Class 1st Commission was setup on January 1979. The commission was chaired by an MP B.P. Mandal. The commission submitted its report to the President on 31st December 1980. The 11 indicators were applied to declared a socially and educationally backwards some are given : (1) Castes / classes considered socially backward by others. (2) Castes / classes that relied on manual labour for their livelihood. (3) Castes / classes where participation of women in work is at least 25% more than the state average. (4) Castes or classes amongst whom the proportion of matriculates is at least 25% less than the state average.

question: Assertion (A) : The five permanent members of the Security Council have been given the veto power. Reason (R) : Since the concurrence of live permanent members is essential, they can use veto power even in procedural matters.

options.a: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.b: Both A and R are true but R is not a correct explanation of A
options.c: A is true but R is false
options.d: A is false but R is true

explanation: The United Nations security Council "power of veto" refers to the veto power wielded solely by the five permanent members of the United Nations Security Council (China, France, Russia, United Kingdom, and United States), enabling them to prevent the adoption of any "substantive" resolution. Abstention or absence from the vote by a permanent member does not prevent a draft resolution from being adopted. The permanent members can vote against a "procedural" draft resolution without blocking its adoption by the Council. A negative vote by a permanent member will also block the selection of a secretary general, although this is a "recommendation" to the General Assembly rather than a Resolution.

question: What is the name for an election victory where one side wins most of the votes?
explanation: A landslide victory is an election result in which the victorious candidate or party wins by an overwhelming margin. The term became popular in the 1800s to describe a victory in which the opposition is "buried", similar to the way in which a geological landslide buries whatever is in its path.

question: Which state in India has common civil code
explanation: Uniform Civil Code is defined in our Constitution under Article 44 which states that it is the duty of the states to secure for the citizens a uniform civil code throughout the territory of India. • It is the common set of governing rules for all citizens of India which refers to replace the personal laws. • Goa is the only state which has a Uniform Civil Code.

question: Which of the following statements is/are correct? 1. District Collector directs and supervises the revenue department of the district 2. District Superintendent of Police grants licenses for arms explosives in the district Select the correct answer using the codes given below : Polity
explanation: District Collector is the executive head of the district with numerous responsibilities in the sphere of revenue administration, civil administration, development, panchayats, local bodies, etc. The District collector is the highest officer of revenue administration in the district. In revenue matters, he/she is responsible to the Government through the Division commissioner and chairman, Board of revenue. He is responsible for the collection of the land revenue, other kinds of government taxes, fees and all dues, recoverable as arrears of land revenue. District collector also grants licenses under Arms Rules 1962.

question: Name of a controversial Minister of Delhi Gov't. who allegedly barged into a house where some African women were living in January 2014 is :
explanation: Somnath Bharti was a controversial Minister of Delhi Government who allegedly barged into a house where some African women were living in January. This case was related to four African women two Nigerians and two Ugandan. Saket court of Delhi ordered Delhi Police to Lodge an FIR on the complaint of the African women, who later identified Bharti as being the man who committed the alleged excesses.

question: The two ordinances which the President of India declined to promulgate for reasons of Constitutional propriety on the eve of the election to the 11th Lok Sabha related to

question: Which of the following are Acts against which write jurisdiction is not available : 1. M I S A 2. N S A 3. E S M A Choose the correct answer from the codes given below :
explanation: MISA & NSA– The Maintenance of Internal Security Act was a controversial law passed by the Indian Parliament in 1971. NSA: National Security Act is an act that Empowers the government to detain a person if the authorities are satisfied that he/she is a threat to national security or to preventing him/her from disrupting public order. ESMA: Essentials Services Maintenance Act was established to ensure the delivery of certain services which if obstructed would affect the normal life of the people of India. Writ Jurisdiction is not available against MISA & NSA acts.

question: Which one of the following States was 10th unrepresented in the Lok Sabha?
explanation: Jammu and Kashmir State was unrepresented in the 10th Lok Sabha (20 June 1991-10 May 1996) during Insurgency in Jammu and Kashmir (Started in late 1989) the Armed Forces (Jammu and Kashmir) Special Powers Act, 1990 was enacted in July 1990. Note:- Jammu and Kashmir Reorganization Act, 2019 was passed by both the houses (Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha) of the Indian Parliament in August 2019. After this re-Constitution the former state divided into two Union Territories, Jammu and Kashmir (with legislative assembly) and Ladakh.

question: Whose duty is it to cause to be laid before the Parliament the Annual Financial Statement (popularly known as Budget)?

options.d: The union minister of Parliamentary Affairs

explanation: The Annual Financial Statement is caused to be laid before both the houses of Parliament by the President. Article 112 of the Indian Constitution says that every year, the President of India shall cause to laid before both the houses of the parliament the Annual Financial statement. This is popularly known as budget. • The budget gives the complete picture of the estimated receipts and expenditures of the government of India for that year.

question: With reference to ASEAN, consider the following statements. 1. ASEAN was initially formed by the governments of five South Asian countries. 2. It was formed in August, 1967 through the Bangkok Declaration. Which of the statements given above is/are correct? Polity
explanation: ASEAN : The Association of Southeast Asian Nations, or ASEAN, was established on 8 August 1967 in Bangkok, Thailand, with the signing of the ASEAN Declaration (Bangkok Declaration) namely Indonesia, Malaysia, Philippines, Singapore and Thailand. Currently, there are 10 permanent members of this association. Its headquarter is in Jakarta (Indonesia). The association of Southeast Asian Nations is a regional intergovernmental organization comprising ten countries in Southeast Asia. Aim – It main aim to promotes intergovernmental cooperation and facilitates economic, political, security, military, educational and sociocultural integration among its members and other countries in Asia. • India could not become a member of this association due to its geographical location. India is a part of South-Asia while ASEAN is an organization of South- East-Asia nations on July 23, 1996, ASEAN gave advisory status to India.

question: Consider the following : 1. Indian Board of Wildlife 2. Finance Commission 3. National Development Council 4. Planning Commission Which of the above is/are headed by the Prime Minister of India?
explanation: Indian Board of Wildlife, National Development Council and Planning Commission (now NITI Aayog formed on 1 Jan 2015) are headed by the Prime Minister of India. • Finance Commission is a Constitutional body for the purpose of allocation of certain revenue resources between union and the state governments, established under Article 280 of the Indian Constitution. • NDC is the apex body established in 1952 for decision making and deliberations on development matters in India. • Planning Commission, although no longer active (Since it was replaced by the NITI Aayog), was an important body for policy making and governance in India. Note:- The National Board for Wildlife(NBWL) is the apex body in India for all wildlife-related matters. It is a statutory body constituted under section 5A of the Wildlife Protection Act, 1972.
